main_event: Ten groups of unidentified lights in straight east-west flight observed over Nebraska missile sites on 26 August 1965 by military and civilian witnesses.
sighted_object: Objects size of North Star, pale yellow to white; luminous glow, semi-circular in shape with trailing white glow; six red, green, and white lights; oblong saucer-sized object with flashing light
course: East to West & West to East
witness_description: Nine military personnel at Hotel launch facility with binoculars; three airmen of Geodetic Survey Team with theodolite; Staff Sergeant and two neighbors; group of eight young civilians with 50/100 telescope; civilian guard at Sioux Army Depot; Air Force personnel at Golf Launch Control
interesting_points: Objects reappeared at four 15-minute intervals on the same course at 20,000 feet, very fast
On four occasions there was a roaring jet-like noise at some distance behind
Theodolite observation: single object became three white objects grouped; on successive observations became six red, green, and white lights
Object at Golf launch site identified as Jupiter by theodolite observation of three moons of that planet
Staff Sergeant's oblong saucer-sized object excluded as weather balloon: balloons launched at 10AM and 10PM
Object also observed on 31 August and 3 September
Report compiled by Major Elva D. Brown, Intelligence Staff Officer, 19 years of military experience
